acceptance_targets: [chapter_16_award_01] Pass if the revised material replacing the late chapter around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:149 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:175 leaves Gavin with a concrete new condition by the end of the chapter that is visible on the page and not merely anticipated. Fail if the chapter still closes with Gavin essentially continuing the same work under the same conditions, only more aware that battle is coming.

[chapter_16_craft_01] Pass if at least one revised passage anchored to snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:9 or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:153 shows a specific human cost or physical disturbance unfolding in-scene and leaving sensory residue after the moment. Fail if those sections still mostly summarize categories of unrest or approaching conflict without an embodied consequence the reader can point to.

[chapter_16_prose_01] Pass if the revised chapter contains no more than two 'the way you...' similes and no more than two paragraph-opening 'Not X ...' contrast turns, with each remaining instance doing distinct work in a distinct context. Fail if those templates are still the chapter's dominant explanatory rhythm across the cited passages.

[full_award_006] Pass if the revised line at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3386 conveys the outside as impersonal and overwhelming without invoking sea, shoreline, water level, or sand. Fail if the chapter still reaches for another water-based explanation of the same idea.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised material replacing the late chapter around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:149 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:175 leaves Gavin with a concrete new condition by the end of the chapter that is visible on the page and not merely anticipated. Fail if the chapter still closes with Gavin essentially continuing the same work under the same conditions, only more aware that battle is coming.
findings.problem: The chapter's biggest movements are still preparatory rather than fully consequential on the page. School-wide revolt and the eve-of-battle turn are framed as pressure around Gavin's work, so the installment reads like strong setup for chapter 17 instead of a chapter that lands its own irreversible ordeal.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild one of the background-pressure blocks at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:9, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:21, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:149, or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:167 into a present-time collision that changes Gavin's available options before the chapter ends. Keep the broken-wrist reveal, but give the late chapter a concrete turn: an interruption he refuses, a damaged workspace, a lost route, an exposed secret, or another immediate cost that leaves him materially worse off than he was at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:117.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if at least one revised passage anchored to snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:9 or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:153 shows a specific human cost or physical disturbance unfolding in-scene and leaving sensory residue after the moment. Fail if those sections still mostly summarize categories of unrest or approaching conflict without an embodied consequence the reader can point to.
findings.problem: The chapter keeps its promised human rupture at observational distance. It names revolt, hiding students, rumors, and incoming fighters, but those beats arrive mainly as overview and substrate commentary rather than embodied social or physical consequence. In a chapter whose texture is meant to be hot and unavoidable, that summary-buffer reads as sanitization drift: scene pressure is reported instead of fully present.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:9, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:11, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:153, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:167, trade some report language for one rendered aftermath or interruption with sensory residue still on the page: a student in shock, a failed Carrow command landing on actual bodies, scorched stone still smoking, triage supplies being used, dust shaking loose into the sub-collection, or another concrete cost Gavin has to register in-scene. Gavin can stay emotionally alien; the prose cannot stay this buffered.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised chapter contains no more than two 'the way you...' similes and no more than two paragraph-opening 'Not X ...' contrast turns, with each remaining instance doing distinct work in a distinct context. Fail if those templates are still the chapter's dominant explanatory rhythm across the cited passages.
findings.problem: Two explanatory templates dominate the chapter: repeated 'the way you...' analogic similes and repeated 'Not X ...' contrast openings. Used once or twice, each would sharpen Gavin's perception; used this often, they become audible scaffolding and flatten the prose into an autopilot rhythm.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the repeated template clusters at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:5, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:17, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:37, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:75, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:145, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:155, with attention also to nearby repeats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:9, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:35, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:119. Keep only the instances whose comparison does irreplaceable work; convert the others into direct sensory statement, a different sentence architecture, or a less pre-signaled turn of thought.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised line at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3386 conveys the outside as impersonal and overwhelming without invoking sea, shoreline, water level, or sand. Fail if the chapter still reaches for another water-based explanation of the same idea.
findings.problem: Full-book recurring pattern: the outside/ward relation is rendered through hydrological metaphor in 5 chapters (chapters 08, 13, 16, 17, 18). By the time this chapter compares the outside to a rising sea against a drawing in sand, the metaphor has become a reusable template rather than a discovery. REPLACE this chapter's instance.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the comparison at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3386 so the battle-prelude paragraph stops using sea/sand imagery. Keep the same large-scale indifference and inevitability, but recast it in a non-hydrological domain that better fits this chapter's tonal pressure: vibration, resonance, structural load, or another concrete system Gavin has been studying in the wards.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's central risk is tonal: it must sustain dark comedy, cosmic horror, and genuine emotional tenderness in the same narrative without any register canceling the others. Lean into the discomfort of juxtaposition. A scene where Gavin is mentally modeling ward-substrate decay while a classmate screams under the Cruciatus Curse is not a failure of empathy — it is the novel's core tension made visceral. The humor of the premise — that Voldemort's war is an irritating distraction from the real problem — must never be defused by making Gavin feel appropriately guilty at the right moments. He should feel guilty sometimes and not other times, and the reader should sit with the discomfort of a protagonist whose priorities have become genuinely alien. The cosmic material should be rendered with enough specificity that it is genuinely unsettling, not just philosophically interesting. Do not soften the ending: Gavin's refusal to intervene in the Battle of Hogwarts must be played straight, without a last-minute redemptive gesture or moral safety net.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism in this novel is a particularly acute failure because the entire thematic engine runs on the difference between seeing something directly and not seeing it. Gavin's journey is toward unmediated perception. The prose must mirror that commitment. When the Carrows torture a student, render the torture. When Gavin is hit with the Cruciatus, render the pain in his body. When the cosmic threat presses against perception, render what it feels like — do not retreat to abstractions about 'something vast' or 'an incomprehensible presence.' Find the specific sensory detail that makes the incomprehensible land. Euphemism in a novel about the failure to see what is real is thematic self-sabotage.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all dialogue. Uncontracted forms are reserved for deliberate emphasis, Babbling's academic register, or moments where a character is being pointedly formal. If a line reads stiffly, check contractions first.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate chapter textures aggressively. A high-intensity chapter (Cruciatus detention, Duncan's torture) must be followed by a chapter with a different pressure profile — quieter, more internal, more comic, or more focused on intellectual work. No two consecutive chapters should share the same dominant emotional register, the same scene-structure (e.g., two chapters that each build to a confrontation), or the same pacing profile. Vary scene count, dialogue density, and the ratio of interiority to action. Some chapters should be mostly one extended scene; others should move across multiple locations and timeframes. The Christmas chapter and the work-routine chapters should breathe differently from the Carrow chapters and the battle chapter.
prose_style_profile.diction: Plain, concrete, specific. Anglo-Saxon roots preferred over Latinate where both are available. Avoid poetic diction, literary allusion, and metaphors that call attention to their own cleverness. Metaphors should arise from Gavin's actual frame of reference: schoolwork, the castle, ordinary physical experience. When the First Art requires description, reach for precision over beauty — describe what it looks like, not what it means. The novel's most striking language should come from the collision between ordinary diction and extraordinary perception.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, locked to Gavin's perception throughout. The narrative voice has access to his thoughts, feelings, and sensory experience but not to anyone else's interiority. Other characters are known only through what Gavin observes. As the First Art restructures Gavin's cognition, the narrative's perceptual range should gradually expand — he begins noticing things no one else can see — but the voice remains grounded in his ordinary, slightly bewildered perspective. The narration should never become more articulate or literary than Gavin himself would be; the strangeness of what he perceives should press against the ordinariness of how he describes it.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied and responsive to scene pressure. Default rhythm is medium-paced prose with sentences of mixed length — some short, some compound, none habitually long. During scenes of intellectual discovery, let sentences extend as Gavin's attention extends, then cut short when comprehension fails. During Carrow scenes, prose should tighten and become more physical. During quiet scenes, allow room for observation and sensory detail without rushing toward the next event. Do not develop a signature rhythm that persists across all chapters — the rhythm should be the scene's rhythm, not the author's.
